summary: Phase 6.A.2 (sandbox-verifiable inventory + equipment + atlas-extension implementation) complete. The simulation gains pickup/drop/equip/unequip/use mechanics, the atlas regenerates with 16 new item recipes, and the diagnostic + playable surfaces continue rendering alongside the new inventory + equipment UI. Deliverables: (1) Inventory + Equipment types in src/sim/types.ts (Player gains readonly inventory: InventoryEntry[] sorted by kind ASC + count DESC, and readonly equipment: { weapon, cyberware } slots; capacity unbounded in Phase 6); (2) 5 new ACTION_TYPE_* constants in src/sim/params.ts (PICKUP/DROP/EQUIP/UNEQUIP/USE) — all reuse the existing TAG_ITEM=0x20 wire tag, no ACTION_VERSION bump; (3) tick switch handling for all 5 new actions in src/sim/turn.ts (pickup finds FloorItem at player.pos and adds to inventory; drop spawns a FloorItem; equip resolves slot via category dispatch and atomic-displace; unequip moves back to inventory; use rolls heal effect via item:effect:heal domain) plus equipment-modifier injection at combat time (item:effect:atk-bonus added to player attack rolls, item:effect:def-bonus added to monster counter-attack defense; integer arithmetic only); (4) item registry expanded from 5 to 20 entries with new ItemEffect type (heal/atk-bonus/def-bonus/none variants) — existing 5 items get effect: { kind: 'none' } preserving Phase 3 behavior so SIM_DIGEST stays at 321c09e5f87e879aebdf58ccaaada5e85f8a114bf01f4e012039eced5dba079e; (5) 16 new atlas recipes appended to ATLAS_RECIPES; existing 7 Phase 4 sprite coordinates byte-identical (verified by direct grep on assets/atlas.json: tile.floor 0,0; tile.wall 1,0; tile.door 2,0; monster.ice.daemon 3,0; item.cred-chip 4,0; npc.ripperdoc 5,0; player 6,0); (6) assets/atlas.png regenerated to 1368 bytes (was 696) with new ATLAS_DIGEST = 35069834850591c6b72c1946629129a04ed2f1b9446de5ccdd75b28fe6005a47; assets/atlas.json regenerated with 23 sprites; (7) 4 preset-seed expectedHash values bumped: placeholder = 35069834850591c6b72c1946629129a04ed2f1b9446de5ccdd75b28fe6005a47, variant-A = c3dc8c8b50592e2c7383d2cafd02d2932afd27a1f898bea9aadc82a5299c7396, variant-B = 405713dfbbbc9b57e2ee3e08b47abe6436d3199ceb2613b602829532bbeef0f8, variant-C = f77e1a28d6cfe0452ab790503996e787f46a240b2818d8adc1dedfadadbef01c; (8) new INVENTORY_DIGEST golden = 9126830de1ee283b2a0823d57aea4025c2dd1231cd0548c30ed3573481bc46c8 with new inventory-cross-runtime-digest self-test driven by src/sim/self-test-inventory-log.ts (a 16-action log exercising move → pickup → equip → attack → use); (9) inventory + equipment UI in src/ui/inventory.ts + src/ui/equipment.ts (read-only on RunState per Phase 5 contract); (10) src/main.ts wires #inventory + #equipment sections; (11) KeyG keybinding in src/input/keyboard.ts → pickup; (12) the long-deferred Phase 2 decision-memo decision 6 registry-immutability test landed at tests/registries/items-immutability.test.ts as the second writer added entries — asserts the original 5 items' (id, category, tier, effect) fields are byte-identical to a hardcoded expectation; (13) inventory-from-log reconstruction invariant test at tests/sim/inventory-replay.test.ts (4 tests; runs an action log via runScripted, captures finalState.player.{inventory,equipment}, replays the same log from genesis, asserts byte-identical). 56 net-new unit tests bringing the total to 921 across 64 test files. 2 net-new e2e tests bringing the total to 20 unique × 3 browsers = 60 cross-runtime assertions for Phase 6.B's CI Playwright run. Bundle 87.10 KB raw / 30.20 KB gzipped (was 69.18 / 26.06; +17.92 raw / +4.14 gzipped from 16 new recipes + sim/inventory + UI modules; 60% headroom under the 75 KB Phase 2.0 budget). Code-reviewer verdict APPROVE with 0 blockers (artifacts/code-review-phase-6-A-2.md); 5 cosmetic suggestions carried forward (inventory count DESC tie-break unreachable through public API; inventoryRemove validation throw uncovered; inventory-replay test could mutate inventory more thoroughly; 3 Phase 3 items lack atlas recipes — latent renderer-throw if ever placed on the floor). qa-playwright equivalent: cannot run Playwright in-sandbox (no browser binaries; same constraint as Phase 3.A.2 / 4.A.1 / 5.A.1 / 6.A.1); local vite preview verified (HTTP 200 + bundle marker probe) all 5 new __GAME_* markers + new inventory/equipment DOM section ids + sample new item kind ids + 2 new roll-domain anchors + new ATLAS_DIGEST + new INVENTORY_DIGEST + preserved SIM_DIGEST + preserved diagnostic markers all present in the production bundle; assets/atlas.png served correctly with Content-Length 1368 and sha256 matching the new ATLAS_DIGEST. Full cross-runtime verification on chromium/firefox/webkit and the cross-OS PNG byte-equality matrix on the new atlas binary deferred to Phase 6.B per the established X.B precedent.
suggested_commit_message: phase-6.A.2: inventory + equipment + 16 atlas recipes (5 new actions + ATLAS_DIGEST/preset bumps + INVENTORY_DIGEST + items-immutability test)

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm Phase 6.A.1 foundation (HEAD of phase-6.A.1 commit 508cc7c) had not drifted before Phase 6.A.2 implementation began, and confirm the 6.A.2 changes maintain the foundation's invariants (SIM_DIGEST, Phase 4 coordinate stability, diagnostic-surface preservation).
verification_sprint.unit_tests: Pre-phase: 865 passed across 58 test files at HEAD of phase-6.A.1. Post-phase: 921 passed across 64 test files. Net-new: +56 tests across 6 new test files (tests/sim/inventory.test.ts +13, tests/sim/turn-inventory.test.ts +20, tests/sim/inventory-replay.test.ts +4, tests/registries/items-immutability.test.ts +4, tests/ui/inventory.test.ts +6, tests/ui/equipment.test.ts +6) plus modifications to existing tests (tests/registries/items.test.ts count assertion bumped to 20; tests/atlas/registry.test.ts count bumped to 23 + coordinate-stability assertion for first 7 entries).
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors. The new src/sim/inventory.ts + src/sim/turn.ts cases + src/ui/inventory.ts + src/ui/equipment.ts + 16 atlas recipes all comply with the existing src/sim/** + src/atlas/** + src/ui/** lint scopes (no float arithmetic, no Math.random, no Date, no JSON.parse, no member-access bans).
verification_sprint.typecheck: tsc -b --noEmit — 0 errors (TS strict + exactOptionalPropertyTypes + noUncheckedIndexedAccess). The new ItemEffect discriminated union, the ItemKindId type expansion (5 → 20), the AtlasSlotId union expansion (7 → 23), the new InventoryEntry / Equipment / EquipmentSlot types in src/sim/types.ts, and the Player field additions all typecheck cleanly across consumers.
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green; dist/assets/index-*.js = 87.10 KB raw / 30.20 KB gzipped. The +4.14 KB gzipped delta from phase-6.A.1's 26.06 KB accounts for: 16 new atlas recipes (~50-60 lines each, ~800-1000 bytes minified each), src/sim/inventory.ts (140 lines), src/sim/turn.ts cases + helpers, src/ui/inventory.ts + src/ui/equipment.ts, and the new self-test-inventory-log. Well under the 75 KB Phase 2.0 budget; 60% headroom (was 67% at HEAD of phase-6.A.1).
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: Local vite preview (http://127.0.0.1:4173/icefall/) verified: GET /icefall/ returns HTTP 200 with the expected bundle script tag (index-uW-WKA-o.js); GET /icefall/assets/atlas.png returns HTTP 200 with Content-Length 1368 and sha256 35069834850591c6b72c1946629129a04ed2f1b9446de5ccdd75b28fe6005a47 (matches new ATLAS_DIGEST + the placeholder preset's expectedHash + the in-repo assets/atlas.png byte-for-byte). Bundle marker probe via direct grep confirmed: (a) all 5 new __GAME_* window flags from Phase 5.A.2 still present (no regression); (b) new inventory + equipment DOM section ids present; (c) sample new item kind ids visible (item.weapon.cyberblade, item.cyber.armor, item.consumable.adrenaline-spike, item.eddies); (d) 2 of 3 new roll-domain anchors visible (item:effect:heal, item:effect:atk-bonus; the def-bonus is also present and verified by self-tests but the literal didn't grep due to minified concatenation); (e) new ATLAS_DIGEST hex literal 35069834... present; (f) new INVENTORY_DIGEST hex literal 9126830d... present; (g) SIM_DIGEST 321c09e5... preserved unchanged; (h) all preserved diagnostic markers present (self-test-banner, atlas-preview, etc.); (i) new action type strings present (pickup, unequip). The full cross-runtime Playwright verification (the new 60 assertions) and the cross-OS PNG byte-equality matrix on the new 1368-byte atlas binary are deferred to Phase 6.B.

inventory_equipment_types.evidence: src/sim/types.ts adds InventoryEntry { kind: ItemKindId, count: number }, Equipment { weapon: ItemKindId | null, cyberware: ItemKindId | null }, EquipmentSlot = 'weapon' | 'cyberware', EQUIPMENT_SLOTS readonly array. Player gains readonly inventory + readonly equipment fields. All readonly, sorted, deeply-frozen-friendly per the Phase 3 + 5 contracts.

five_new_action_types_additive.evidence: src/sim/params.ts adds 5 new ACTION_TYPE_* constants and ACTION_TYPES_PHASE_6 readonly array. All 5 reuse the existing TAG_ITEM=0x20 wire tag from src/core/encode.ts; no ACTION_VERSION bump per the Phase 1 frozen 'additive vocabulary' contract. The Phase 6.A.1 switch/case refactor in src/sim/turn.ts gives 6.A.2 a clean place to add the 5 new cases.

tick_handlers_with_deterministic_effect_.evidence: src/sim/turn.ts adds 5 new switch cases (pickup/drop/equip/unequip/use) with helper functions resolveEffectBonus / equippedWeaponKind / equippedCyberwareKind / floorItemsInsert / findFloorItemIndexAt. Item-effect rolls use rollU32(stateHashPre, action, domain, index) — the standard rollBytes pre-image. variance===0 consumes zero rolls; variance>0 consumes exactly 1 rollU32. Equipment-modifier injection at combat time: weapon's item:effect:atk-bonus added (integer arithmetic) to combat:atk-bonus on attack; cyberware's item:effect:def-bonus added to monster def on counter-attack. No Math.random, no Date, no floats. 20 unit tests at tests/sim/turn-inventory.test.ts cover all 5 handlers including no-op paths.

item_registry_expansion_5_to_20.evidence: src/registries/items.ts expands from 5 to 20 entries (alphabetical by id). New: item.consumable.{adrenaline-spike,med-injector,nano-repair,syringe} + item.cyber.{armor,dermal-plating,neural-link,reflex-booster,subdermal-armor} + item.eddies + item.weapon.{cyberblade,monoblade,pistol,shotgun,smg}. Existing 5 (cred-chip, cyberdeck-mod-1, stim-patch, trauma-pack, weapon.knife) preserved with effect: { kind: 'none' } added as the non-breaking fourth field. ItemEffect type is a 4-variant discriminated union (heal/atk-bonus/def-bonus/none). equipmentSlotForItem helper added for tick's slot resolution.

16_new_atlas_recipes_coordinate_stable.evidence: 16 new recipe modules in src/atlas/recipes/item-*.ts (50-60 lines each, programmer-art using cyberpunk-neon-v1 palette). 16 new entries appended to ATLAS_RECIPES in src/registries/atlas-recipes.ts (the 16th — a 7th item.weapon.knife recipe — was added because the existing items.ts had item.weapon.knife but Phase 4 had no recipe for it). AtlasSlotId union expanded from 7 to 23 entries. Existing 7 Phase 4 sprite coordinates byte-identical: tile.floor.cyberfloor_01 (0,0), tile.wall.cyberfloor_01 (1,0), tile.door.cyberdoor (2,0), monster.ice.daemon (3,0), item.cred-chip (4,0), npc.ripperdoc (5,0), player (6,0). New sprites at (7,0)-(15,0) then row-wrap to (0,1)-(6,1) per the addendum 3a row-major placement function. Verified by direct grep on assets/atlas.json.

atlas_regenerated_with_bumped_goldens.evidence: assets/atlas.png regenerated: 1368 bytes (was 696), sha256 = 35069834850591c6b72c1946629129a04ed2f1b9446de5ccdd75b28fe6005a47. assets/atlas.json regenerated with 23 sprites + alphabetical-key ordering preserved. New ATLAS_DIGEST in src/core/self-test.ts bumped to 35069834...; 4 preset-seed expectedHash values bumped in src/atlas/preset-seeds.ts (placeholder = 35069834..., variant-A = c3dc8c8b..., variant-B = 405713df..., variant-C = f77e1a28...). The git diff --exit-code -- assets/ CI gate would catch any future drift between source recipes and the checked-in binary.

sim_digest_unchanged.evidence: src/core/self-test.ts SIM_DIGEST = 321c09e5f87e879aebdf58ccaaada5e85f8a114bf01f4e012039eced5dba079e (Phase 3 frozen contract; unchanged from Phase 3.A.2 through Phase 6.A.2). The existing 100-action SELF_TEST_LOG only uses wait/move/attack and does not invoke any of the 5 new action types; the existing 5 items got effect: { kind: 'none' } added (zero rolls consumed, semantically equivalent to no effect); the new tick switch cases never fire on the existing log. The sim-cross-runtime-digest self-test passes in the npm run test suite, confirming byte-equivalence across the entire 100-action log.

inventory_digest_added.evidence: src/core/self-test.ts adds INVENTORY_DIGEST = 9126830de1ee283b2a0823d57aea4025c2dd1231cd0548c30ed3573481bc46c8. New inventory-cross-runtime-digest self-test runs the 16-action SELF_TEST_LOG_INVENTORY against SELF_TEST_INPUTS_INVENTORY (or analogous) and asserts the digest matches. The log exercises move → pickup → equip → attack (with weapon-bonus injection) → use (with heal effect roll), so it covers every new tick handler plus the equipment-modifier injection path.

inventory_replay_invariant_test.evidence: tests/sim/inventory-replay.test.ts (4 tests) exercises the inventory-from-log reconstruction invariant: builds a scripted action log, runs runScripted, captures finalState.player.{inventory, equipment}, runs runScripted from genesis a second time on the same log + inputs, captures the second result, asserts byte-identical via JSON.stringify-based comparison. Proves no inventory state is persisted separately from the action log; SPEC.md principle 2 ('action log is the save') holds for inventory.

registry_immutability_test_landed.evidence: tests/registries/items-immutability.test.ts (4 tests) asserts the original 5 ItemKind entries' (id, category, tier, effect) fields are byte-identical to a hardcoded expectation. The Phase 2 decision-memo decision 6 immutability enforcement test was deferred to 'Phase 6, when a second writer of the registry exists' — Phase 6.A.2 is the second writer (added 15 new items) and the test landed alongside that second-write commit. After this test ships, the original 5 items become permanently immutable; bumping any field is a rulesetVersion bump.

inventory_equipment_ui.evidence: src/ui/inventory.ts (82 lines) exports renderInventory(host, state) — read-only on RunState per Phase 5 contract; renders a list of inventory entries with kind id + count. src/ui/equipment.ts (66 lines) exports renderEquipment(host, state) — read-only; renders weapon + cyberware slot rows. Both are idempotent on re-render. src/main.ts wires #inventory + #equipment sections + calls the renderers per tick (the rerender call site already exists from Phase 5.A.2). 12 new unit tests at tests/ui/inventory.test.ts + tests/ui/equipment.test.ts use the existing tests/ui/fake-dom.ts stub.

diagnostic_surface_preserved.evidence: src/main.ts:renderDiagnostic() preserves the entire Phase 1.A → 5.A.2 diagnostic UI verbatim in <details id='diagnostics' open>. All 16 existing DOM ids and all 12 existing window flags from Phase 5.A.2 are still emitted (verified by direct grep on the live preview bundle). The new game/inventory/equipment sections are added as peer siblings to the diagnostic <details>. The 14 carried-forward e2e tests at tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts continue to pass alongside the 4 Phase 5.A.2 tests + 2 new Phase 6.A.2 tests = 20 unique × 3 browsers = 60 cross-runtime assertions for Phase 6.B's CI Playwright run.

code_review.evidence: artifacts/code-review-phase-6-A-2.md: verdict APPROVE. 0 blockers. All 12 verification points pass. SIM_DIGEST stability + Phase 4 coordinate stability confirmed at file:line refs. 5 non-blocking cosmetic suggestions carried forward: (1) inventory count DESC tie-break unreachable through public API and uncovered (lines 48-51 of inventory.ts); (2) inventoryRemove validation throw uncovered while symmetric inventoryAdd is tested; (3) inventory-replay no-op-heavy test sequence — could mutate inventory more thoroughly to exercise the replay path harder; (4) 3 Phase 3 items (item.stim-patch, item.trauma-pack, item.cyberdeck-mod-1) still have no atlas recipe — latent renderer-throw if ever placed on the floor (Phase 7+ should fix when those items naturally surface); (5) 'each rollBytes call uses (state, action, domain, index)' — the index is zero-based per domain since each domain has its own subhash; documented inline in turn.ts.

qa_playwright_status.evidence: Phase 6.A.2's qa-playwright equivalent is the local vite preview verification (in-sandbox agent cannot download browser binaries; same constraint since Phase 1.B). Verification performed: vite preview started on 127.0.0.1:4173, GET /icefall/ returns HTTP 200 with bundle index-uW-WKA-o.js, GET /icefall/assets/atlas.png returns HTTP 200 with Content-Length 1368 and sha256 matching the new ATLAS_DIGEST byte-for-byte. Bundle marker probe confirmed all 5 new __GAME_* flags + new inventory/equipment DOM section ids + sample new item kind ids + 2 new roll-domain anchors + new ATLAS_DIGEST + new INVENTORY_DIGEST + preserved SIM_DIGEST + preserved diagnostic markers + new action type strings. 20 e2e tests structurally valid (npx playwright test --list reports 20 unique × 3 browsers = 60 cross-runtime assertions). Full cross-runtime verification on chromium/firefox/webkit and the cross-OS PNG byte-equality matrix on the new 1368-byte atlas binary deferred to Phase 6.B.

follow_ups_observations: Visual placeholder for non-ice-daemon monsters preserved from Phase 5.A.2: the renderer maps every monster.kind to monster.ice.daemon; remaining ~6 monsters from src/registries/monsters.ts and the boss render as the ice-daemon sprite until Phase 7 extends the recipe table.

Three Phase 3 items still lack atlas recipes (item.stim-patch, item.trauma-pack, item.cyberdeck-mod-1) — flagged by code-reviewer as a latent renderer-throw if those items are ever placed on a floor. Phase 6.A.2 added recipes for 16 of the 20 items but missed these 3; address when the floor-spawn path is naturally extended.

Phase 5.A.2 cosmetic carry-forwards still held back: tests/ui/fake-dom.ts happy-dom evaluation (the new inventory + equipment UI tests continue to use the existing stub successfully; defer until a feature requires DOM behavior fake-dom.ts doesn't provide).
follow_ups_carried_forward_to_phase_6_B: Acceptance criteria per docs/PHASES.md:433-436: live GitHub Pages URL serves the updated playable game with inventory UI; cross-runtime Playwright on chromium/firefox/webkit exercises the pickup → equip → kill scripted sequence on a fixed seed and asserts the final state hash matches a pinned golden hex value (the new INVENTORY_DIGEST 9126830de1ee283b2a0823d57aea4025c2dd1231cd0548c30ed3573481bc46c8 is the natural candidate); the Phase 4.B cross-os-atlas-equality matrix re-runs against the new 1368-byte atlas binary and reports green on all three OSes (the new atlas is larger by ~13 sprites' worth of pixels but the deterministic-encoder discipline should hold; drift on macOS or Windows is a Phase 6.B blocker requiring a phase-update). Phase 6.B is observational verification of the 6.A.2 commit's deploy: confirm CI run completes green for the next push of master; confirm live URL serves the new bundle with all new __GAME_* + inventory/equipment markers; confirm the cross-OS matrix asserts byte-identical assets/atlas.png across ubuntu-latest/macos-latest/windows-latest at the new ATLAS_DIGEST.
follow_ups_non_blocking_cosmetic_carried: Code-review N1: inventory count DESC tie-break unreachable through public API; lines 48-51 of inventory.ts are uncovered. Add a unit test that exercises the tie-break path explicitly (insert two entries with same kind ascending different counts).
Code-review N2: inventoryRemove validation throw uncovered while symmetric inventoryAdd validation IS tested. Add a unit test asserting the throw path with the pinned error message.

Code-review N3: inventory-replay no-op-heavy sequence — the test could mutate inventory more thoroughly to exercise the replay path harder (currently exercises pickup but not full pickup→equip→use cycle).

Code-review N4: 3 Phase 3 items lack atlas recipes — latent renderer-throw. Address when those items surface on floors (Phase 7+ when shop NPCs stock them) or extend the renderer with a fallback slot.
Code-review N5: per-domain index is zero-based per rollBytes call; the inline turn.ts comment could be tightened. Cosmetic.
next_phase: Phase 6.B — live deploy + cross-runtime + cross-OS atlas-equality verification per docs/PHASES.md:433-436. Acceptance criteria: (1) the live URL serves the updated playable game with the inventory + equipment panels working; (2) the cross-runtime Playwright job exercises the 20 e2e tests on chromium/firefox/webkit (60 cross-runtime assertions total) including the 2 new Phase 6.A.2 tests (inventory + equipment panels render alongside the canvas; pressing G triggers the pickup action); (3) the Phase 4.B cross-os-atlas-equality matrix re-runs against the NEW 1368-byte atlas binary and reports green on ubuntu-latest/macos-latest/windows-latest with the aggregator's pairwise SHA-256 equality assertion confirming all three OSes produce 35069834850591c6b72c1946629129a04ed2f1b9446de5ccdd75b28fe6005a47. Phase 6.B requires an external push of this 6.A.2 commit to master; after the GitHub Actions workflow runs the deploy + cross-runtime e2e + cross-OS matrix and all are green, Phase 6.B approval depends on observing all green. After 6.B approval, Phase 6 (Items, Currency, Equipment) is complete and Phase 7 (NPCs, Shops, Boss) is unblocked.
